name: bitstring

metadata.name: bitstring
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Simple construction, analysis and modification of binary data.

documentation:
![bitstring](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/scott-griffiths/bitstring/main/doc/bitstring_logo_small.png "bitstring")

**bitstring** is a Python module to help make the creation and analysis of all types of bit-level binary data as simple and efficient as possible.

It has been actively maintained since 2006.



[![CI badge](https://github.com/scott-griffiths/bitstring/actions/workflows/.github/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/scott-griffiths/bitstring/actions/workflows/ci.yml)
[![Docs](https://img.shields.io/readthedocs/bitstring?logo=readthedocs&logoColor=white)](https://bitstring.readthedocs.io/en/latest/)
[![Codacy Badge](https://img.shields.io/codacy/grade/8869499b2eed44548fa1a5149dd451f4?logo=codacy)](https://app.codacy.com/gh/scott-griffiths/bitstring/dashboard?utm_source=gh&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=&utm_campaign=Badge_grade)
[![Dependents (via libraries.io)](https://img.shields.io/librariesio/dependents/pypi/bitstring?logo=libraries.io&logoColor=white)](https://libraries.io/pypi/bitstring)
   
[![Pepy Total Downlods](https://img.shields.io/pepy/dt/bitstring?logo=python&logoColor=white&labelColor=blue&color=blue)](https://www.pepy.tech/projects/bitstring)
[![PyPI - Downloads](https://img.shields.io/pypi/dm/bitstring?label=%40&labelColor=blue&color=blue)](https://pypistats.org/packages/bitstring)

----

> [!NOTE]
> To see what been added, improved or fixed, and also to see what's coming in the next version, see the [release notes](https://github.com/scott-griffiths/bitstring/blob/main/release_notes.md).


# Overview

* Efficiently store and manipulate binary data in idiomatic Python.
* Create bitstrings from hex, octal, binary, files, formatted strings, bytes, integers and floats of different endiannesses.
* Powerful binary packing and unpacking functions.
* Bit-level slicing, joining, searching, replacing and more.
* Create and manipulate arrays of fixed-length bitstrings.
* Read from and interpret bitstrings as streams of binary data.
* Rich API - chances are that whatever you want to do there's a simple and elegant way of doing it.
* Open source software, released under the MIT licence.

# Documentation

Extensive documentation for the bitstring module is available.
Some starting points are given below:

* [Overview](https://bitstring.readthedocs.io/en/stable/index.html)
* [Quick Reference](https://bitstring.readthedocs.io/en/stable/quick_reference.html)
* [Full Reference](https://bitstring.readthedocs.io/en/stable/reference.html)

There is also an introductory walkthrough notebook on [binder](https://mybinder.org/v2/gh/scott-griffiths/bitstring/main?labpath=doc%2Fwalkthrough.ipynb).

# Examples

### Installation
```
$ pip install bitstring
```

### Creation
```pycon
>>> from bitstring import Bits, BitArray, BitStream, pack
>>> a = BitArray(bin='00101')
>>> b = Bits(a_file_object)
>>> c = BitArray('0xff, 0b101, 0o65, uint6=22')
>>> d = pack('intle16, hex=a, 0b1', 100, a='0x34f')
>>> e = pack('<16h', *range(16))
```

### Different interpretations, slicing and concatenation
```pycon
>>> a = BitArray('0x3348')
>>> a.hex, a.bin, a.uint, a.float, a.bytes
('3348', '0011001101001000', 13128, 0.2275390625, b'3H')
>>> a[10:3:-1].bin
'0101100'
>>> '0b100' + 3*a
BitArray('0x866906690669, 0b000')
```

### Reading data sequentially
```pycon
>>> b = BitStream('0x160120f')
>>> b.read(12).hex
'160'
>>> b.pos = 0
>>> b.read('uint12')
352
>>> b.readlist('uint12, bin3')
[288, '111']
```

### Searching, inserting and deleting
```pycon
>>> c = BitArray('0b00010010010010001111')   # c.hex == '0x1248f'
>>> c.find('0x48')
(8,)
>>> c.replace('0b001', '0xabc')
>>> c.insert('0b0000', pos=3)
>>> del c[12:16]
```

### Arrays of fixed-length formats
```pycon
>>> from bitstring import Array
>>> a = Array('uint7', [9, 100, 3, 1])
>>> a.data
BitArray('0x1390181')
>>> a[::2] *= 5
>>> a
Array('uint7', [45, 100, 15, 1])
```

functions.name: bitstring.utils.structparser
documentation: Parse struct-like format string token into sub-token list.

functions.name: bitstring.utils.tokenparser
documentation:
Divide the format string into tokens and parse them.

Return stretchy token and list of [initialiser, length, value]
initialiser is one of: hex, oct, bin, uint, int, se, ue, 0x, 0o, 0b etc.
length is None if not known, as is value.

If the token is in the keyword dictionary (keys) then it counts as a
special case and isn't messed with.

tokens must be of the form: [factor*][initialiser][:][length][=value]

classes.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.scale
documentation: The multiplicative scale applied when interpreting the data.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.name
documentation: A string giving the name of the data type.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.length
documentation: The length of the data type in units of bits_per_item. Set to None for variable length dtypes.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.bitlength
documentation: The number of bits needed to represent a single instance of the data type. Set to None for variable length dtypes.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.bits_per_item
documentation: The number of bits for each unit of length. Usually 1, but equals 8 for bytes type.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.variable_length
documentation: If True then the length of the data type depends on the data being interpreted, and must not be specified.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.return_type
documentation: The type of the value returned by the parse method, such as int, float or str.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.is_signed
documentation: If True then the data type represents a signed quantity.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.set_fn
documentation: A function to set the value of the data type.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.get_fn
documentation: A function to get the value of the data type.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.read_fn
documentation: A function to read the value of the data type.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.build
documentation:
Create a bitstring from a value.

The value parameter should be of a type appropriate to the dtype.

methods.name: bitstring.dtypes.Dtype.parse
documentation:
Parse a bitstring to find its value.

The b parameter should be a bitstring of the appropriate length, or an object that can be converted to a bitstring.

A data type class, representing a concrete interpretation of binary data.

Dtype instances are immutable. They are often created implicitly elsewhere via a token string.

```pycon
>>> u12 = Dtype('uint', 12)  # length separate from token string.
>>> float16 = Dtype('float16')  # length part of token string.
>>> mxfp = Dtype('e3m2mxfp', scale=2 ** 6)  # dtype with scaling factor
```

Not (yet) part of the public interface.

classes.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__init__
documentation:
Either specify an 'auto' initialiser:
A string of comma separated tokens, an integer, a file object,
a bytearray, a boolean iterable or another bitstring.

Or initialise via **kwargs with one (and only one) of:
bin -- binary string representation, e.g. '0b001010'.
hex -- hexadecimal string representation, e.g. '0x2ef'
oct -- octal string representation, e.g. '0o777'.
bytes -- raw data as a bytes object, for example read from a binary file.
int -- a signed integer.
uint -- an unsigned integer.
float / floatbe -- a big-endian floating point number.
bool -- a boolean (True or False).
se -- a signed exponential-Golomb code.
ue -- an unsigned exponential-Golomb code.
sie -- a signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
uie -- an unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
floatle -- a little-endian floating point number.
floatne -- a native-endian floating point number.
bfloat / bfloatbe - a big-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatle -- a little-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatne -- a native-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
intbe -- a signed big-endian whole byte integer.
intle -- a signed little-endian whole byte integer.
intne -- a signed native-endian whole byte integer.
uintbe -- an unsigned big-endian whole byte integer.
uintle -- an unsigned little-endian whole byte integer.
uintne -- an unsigned native-endian whole byte integer.
filename -- the path of a file which will be opened in binary read-only mode.

Other keyword arguments:
length -- length of the bitstring in bits, if needed and appropriate.
          It must be supplied for all integer and float initialisers.
offset -- bit offset to the data. These offset bits are
          ignored and this is intended for use when
          initialising using 'bytes' or 'filename'.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.copy
documentation: Return a copy of the bitstring.

Append bs to current bitstring. Return self.

bs -- the bitstring to append.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__copy__
documentation: Return a new copy of the BitArray.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__delitem__
documentation:
Delete item or range.

```pycon
>>> a = BitArray('0x001122')
>>> del a[8:16]
>>> print a
0x0022
```

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__ilshift__
documentation:
Shift bits by n to the left in place. Return self.

n -- the number of bits to shift. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__irshift__
documentation:
Shift bits by n to the right in place. Return self.

n -- the number of bits to shift. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.__imul__
documentation:
Concatenate n copies of self in place. Return self.

Called for expressions of the form 'a *= 3'.
n -- The number of concatenations. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.replace
documentation:
Replace all occurrences of old with new in place.

Returns number of replacements made.

old -- The bitstring to replace.
new -- The replacement bitstring.
start -- Any occurrences that start before this will not be replaced.
         Defaults to 0.
end -- Any occurrences that finish after this will not be replaced.
       Defaults to len(self).
count -- The maximum number of replacements to make. Defaults to
         replace all occurrences.
bytealigned -- If True replacements will only be made on byte
               boundaries.

Raises ValueError if old is empty or if start or end are
out of range.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.insert
documentation:
Insert bs at bit position pos.

bs -- The bitstring to insert.
pos -- The bit position to insert at.

Raises ValueError if pos < 0 or pos > len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.overwrite
documentation:
Overwrite with bs at bit position pos.

bs -- The bitstring to overwrite with.
pos -- The bit position to begin overwriting from.

Raises ValueError if pos < 0 or pos > len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.append
documentation:
Append a bitstring to the current bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to append.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.prepend
documentation:
Prepend a bitstring to the current bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to prepend.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.reverse
documentation:
Reverse bits in-place.

start -- Position of first bit to reverse. Defaults to 0.
end -- One past the position of the last bit to reverse.
       Defaults to len(self).

Using on an empty bitstring will have no effect.

Raises ValueError if start < 0, end > len(self) or end < start.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.set
documentation:
Set one or many bits to 1 or 0.

value -- If bool(value) is True bits are set to 1, otherwise they are set to 0.
pos -- Either a single bit position or an iterable of bit positions.
       Negative numbers are treated in the same way as slice indices.
       Defaults to the entire bitstring.

Raises IndexError if pos < -len(self) or pos >= len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.invert
documentation:
Invert one or many bits from 0 to 1 or vice versa.

pos -- Either a single bit position or an iterable of bit positions.
       Negative numbers are treated in the same way as slice indices.

Raises IndexError if pos < -len(self) or pos >= len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.ror
documentation:
Rotate bits to the right in-place.

bits -- The number of bits to rotate by.
start -- Start of slice to rotate. Defaults to 0.
end -- End of slice to rotate. Defaults to len(self).

Raises ValueError if bits < 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.rol
documentation:
Rotate bits to the left in-place.

bits -- The number of bits to rotate by.
start -- Start of slice to rotate. Defaults to 0.
end -- End of slice to rotate. Defaults to len(self).

Raises ValueError if bits < 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.byteswap
documentation:
Change the endianness in-place. Return number of repeats of fmt done.

fmt -- A compact structure string, an integer number of bytes or
       an iterable of integers. Defaults to 0, which byte reverses the
       whole bitstring.
start -- Start bit position, defaults to 0.
end -- End bit position, defaults to len(self).
repeat -- If True (the default) the byte swapping pattern is repeated
          as much as possible.

methods.name: bitstring.bitarray_.BitArray.clear
documentation: Remove all bits, reset to zero length.

A container holding a mutable sequence of bits.

Subclass of the immutable Bits class. Inherits all of its
methods (except __hash__) and adds mutating methods.

Mutating methods:

append() -- Append a bitstring.
byteswap() -- Change byte endianness in-place.
clear() -- Remove all bits from the bitstring.
insert() -- Insert a bitstring.
invert() -- Flip bit(s) between one and zero.
overwrite() -- Overwrite a section with a new bitstring.
prepend() -- Prepend a bitstring.
replace() -- Replace occurrences of one bitstring with another.
reverse() -- Reverse bits in-place.
rol() -- Rotate bits to the left.
ror() -- Rotate bits to the right.
set() -- Set bit(s) to 1 or 0.

Methods inherited from Bits:

all() -- Check if all specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
any() -- Check if any of specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
copy() -- Return a copy of the bitstring.
count() -- Count the number of bits set to 1 or 0.
cut() -- Create generator of constant sized chunks.
endswith() -- Return whether the bitstring ends with a sub-string.
find() -- Find a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
findall() -- Find all occurrences of a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
fromstring() -- Create a bitstring from a formatted string.
join() -- Join bitstrings together using current bitstring.
pp() -- Pretty print the bitstring.
rfind() -- Seek backwards to find a sub-bitstring.
split() -- Create generator of chunks split by a delimiter.
startswith() -- Return whether the bitstring starts with a sub-bitstring.
tobitarray() -- Return bitstring as a bitarray from the bitarray package.
tobytes() -- Return bitstring as bytes, padding if needed.
tofile() -- Write bitstring to file, padding if needed.
unpack() -- Interpret bits using format string.

Special methods:

Mutating operators are available: [], <<=, >>=, +=, *=, &=, |= and ^=
in addition to the inherited [], ==, !=, +, *, ~, <<, >>, &, | and ^.

Properties:

[GENERATED_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTIONS]

len -- Length of the bitstring in bits.

modules.name: bitstring
documentation:
This package defines classes that simplify bit-wise creation, manipulation and
interpretation of data.

Classes:

Bits -- An immutable container for binary data.
BitArray -- A mutable container for binary data.
ConstBitStream -- An immutable container with streaming methods.
BitStream -- A mutable container with streaming methods.
Array -- An efficient list-like container where each item has a fixed-length binary format.
Dtype -- Encapsulate the data types used in the other classes.

Functions:

pack -- Create a BitStream from a format string.

Data:

options -- Module-wide options.

Exceptions:

Error -- Module exception base class.
CreationError -- Error during creation.
InterpretError -- Inappropriate interpretation of binary data.
ByteAlignError -- Whole byte position or length needed.
ReadError -- Reading or peeking past the end of a bitstring.

https://github.com/scott-griffiths/bitstring
!/usr/bin/env python

methods.name: bitstring._MyModuleType.lsb0

methods.name: bitstring._MyModuleType.lsb0

An opaque way of adding module level properties. Taken from https://peps.python.org/pep-0549/
This is now deprecated. Use the options object directly instead.

functions.name: bitstring.methods.pack
documentation:
Pack the values according to the format string and return a new BitStream.

fmt -- A single string or a list of strings with comma separated tokens

: describing how to create the BitStream.

values -- Zero or more values to pack according to the format.
kwargs -- A dictionary or keyword-value pairs - the keywords used in the

> format string will be replaced with their given value.

Token examples: 'int:12'

: 'uint:8' : 8 bits as an unsigned integer
  'float:64' : 8 bytes as a big-endian float
  'intbe:16' : 2 bytes as a big-endian signed integer
  'uintbe:16' : 2 bytes as a big-endian unsigned integer
  'intle:32' : 4 bytes as a little-endian signed integer
  'uintle:32' : 4 bytes as a little-endian unsigned integer
  'floatle:64': 8 bytes as a little-endian float
  'intne:24' : 3 bytes as a native-endian signed integer
  'uintne:24' : 3 bytes as a native-endian unsigned integer
  'floatne:32': 4 bytes as a native-endian float
  'hex:80' : 80 bits as a hex string
  'oct:9' : 9 bits as an octal string
  'bin:1' : single bit binary string
  'ue' / 'uie': next bits as unsigned exp-Golomb code
  'se' / 'sie': next bits as signed exp-Golomb code
  'bits:5' : 5 bits as a bitstring object
  'bytes:10' : 10 bytes as a bytes object
  'bool' : 1 bit as a bool
  'pad:3' : 3 zero bits as padding

```pycon
>>> s = pack('uint:12, bits', 100, '0xffe')
>>> t = pack(['bits', 'bin:3'], s, '111')
>>> u = pack('uint:8=a, uint:8=b, uint:55=a', a=6, b=44)
```

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.byteswap
documentation:
Change the endianness in-place of all items in the Array.

If the Array format is not a whole number of bytes a ValueError will be raised.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.count
documentation:
Return count of Array items that equal value.

value -- The quantity to compare each Array element to. Type should be appropriate for the Array format.

For floating point types using a value of float('nan') will count the number of elements that are NaN.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.tobytes
documentation:
Return the Array data as a bytes object, padding with zero bits if needed.

Up to seven zero bits will be added at the end to byte align.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.tofile
documentation:
Write the Array data to a file object, padding with zero bits if needed.

Up to seven zero bits will be added at the end to byte align.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.pp
documentation:
Pretty-print the Array contents.

fmt -- Data format string. Defaults to current Array dtype.
width -- Max width of printed lines in characters. Defaults to 120. A single group will always
         be printed per line even if it exceeds the max width.
show_offset -- If True shows the element offset in the first column of each line.
stream -- A TextIO object with a write() method. Defaults to sys.stdout.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.equals
documentation: Return True if format and all Array items are equal.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array._apply_op_to_all_elements
documentation: Apply op with value to each element of the Array and return a new Array

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array._apply_op_to_all_elements_inplace
documentation: Apply op with value to each element of the Array in place.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array._apply_bitwise_op_to_all_elements
documentation: Apply op with value to each element of the Array as an unsigned integer and return a new Array

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array._apply_bitwise_op_to_all_elements_inplace
documentation: Apply op with value to each element of the Array as an unsigned integer in place.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array._promotetype
documentation:
When combining types which one wins?

1. We only deal with types representing floats or integers.
2. One of the two types gets returned. We never create a new one.
3. Floating point types always win against integer types.
4. Signed integer types always win against unsigned integer types.
5. Longer types win against shorter types.
6. In a tie the first type wins against the second type.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__add__
documentation: Add int or float to all elements.
Operators between Arrays or an Array and scalar value

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__and__
documentation: Bitwise operators

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__rmul__
documentation: Reverse operators between a scalar value and an Array

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__rand__
documentation: Reverse operators between a scalar and something that can be a BitArray.

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__lt__
documentation: Comparison operators

methods.name: bitstring.array_.Array.__neg__
documentation: Unary operators

Return an Array whose elements are initialised according to the fmt string.
The dtype string can be typecode as used in the struct module or any fixed-length bitstring
format.

a = Array('>H', [1, 15, 105])
b = Array('int5', [-9, 0, 4])

The Array data is stored compactly as a BitArray object and the Array behaves very like
a list of items of the given format. Both the Array data and fmt properties can be freely
modified after creation. If the data length is not a multiple of the fmt length then the
Array will have 'trailing_bits' which will prevent some methods from appending to the
Array.

Methods:

append() -- Append a single item to the end of the Array.
byteswap() -- Change byte endianness of all items.
count() -- Count the number of occurences of a value.
extend() -- Append new items to the end of the Array from an iterable.
fromfile() -- Append items read from a file object.
insert() -- Insert an item at a given position.
pop() -- Remove and return an item.
pp() -- Pretty print the Array.
reverse() -- Reverse the order of all items.
tobytes() -- Return Array data as bytes object, padding with zero bits at the end if needed.
tofile() -- Write Array data to a file, padding with zero bits at the end if needed.
tolist() -- Return Array items as a list.

Special methods:

Also available are the operators [], ==, !=, +, *, <<, >>, &, |, ^,
plus the mutating operators [], +=, *=, <<=, >>=, &=, |=, ^=.

Properties:

data -- The BitArray binary data of the Array. Can be freely modified.
dtype -- The format string or typecode. Can be freely modified.
itemsize -- The length *in bits* of a single item. Read only.
trailing_bits -- If the data length is not a multiple of the fmt length, this BitArray
                 gives the leftovers at the end of the data.

classes.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__init__
documentation:
Either specify an 'auto' initialiser:
A string of comma separated tokens, an integer, a file object,
a bytearray, a boolean iterable or another bitstring.

Or initialise via **kwargs with one (and only one) of:
bin -- binary string representation, e.g. '0b001010'.
hex -- hexadecimal string representation, e.g. '0x2ef'
oct -- octal string representation, e.g. '0o777'.
bytes -- raw data as a bytes object, for example read from a binary file.
int -- a signed integer.
uint -- an unsigned integer.
float / floatbe -- a big-endian floating point number.
bool -- a boolean (True or False).
se -- a signed exponential-Golomb code.
ue -- an unsigned exponential-Golomb code.
sie -- a signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
uie -- an unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
floatle -- a little-endian floating point number.
floatne -- a native-endian floating point number.
bfloat / bfloatbe - a big-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatle -- a little-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatne -- a native-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
intbe -- a signed big-endian whole byte integer.
intle -- a signed little-endian whole byte integer.
intne -- a signed native-endian whole byte integer.
uintbe -- an unsigned big-endian whole byte integer.
uintle -- an unsigned little-endian whole byte integer.
uintne -- an unsigned native-endian whole byte integer.
filename -- the path of a file which will be opened in binary read-only mode.

Other keyword arguments:
length -- length of the bitstring in bits, if needed and appropriate.
          It must be supplied for all integer and float initialisers.
offset -- bit offset to the data. These offset bits are
          ignored and this is mainly intended for use when
          initialising using 'bytes' or 'filename'.
pos -- Initial bit position, defaults to 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream._setbytepos
documentation: Move to absolute byte-aligned position in stream.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream._getbytepos
documentation: Return the current position in the stream in bytes. Must be byte aligned.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream._setbitpos
documentation: Move to absolute position bit in bitstream.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream._getbitpos
documentation: Return the current position in the stream in bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__copy__
documentation: Return a new copy of the ConstBitStream for the copy module.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__and__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'and' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '&' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__or__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'or' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '|' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__xor__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'xor' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '^' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__add__
documentation:
Concatenate bitstrings and return new bitstring.

bs -- the bitstring to append.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.append
documentation:
Append a bitstring to the current bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to append.

The current bit position will be moved to the end of the BitStream.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__repr__
documentation:
Return representation that could be used to recreate the bitstring.

If the returned string is too long it will be truncated. See __str__().

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.overwrite
documentation:
Overwrite with bitstring at bit position pos.

bs -- The bitstring to overwrite with.
pos -- The bit position to begin overwriting from.

The current bit position will be moved to the end of the overwritten section.
Raises ValueError if pos < 0 or pos > len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.find
documentation:
Find first occurrence of substring bs.

Returns a single item tuple with the bit position if found, or an
empty tuple if not found. The bit position (pos property) will
also be set to the start of the substring if it is found.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
start -- The bit position to start the search. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the last bit to search.

> Defaults to len(self).

bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be

: found on byte boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty, if start < 0, if end > len(self) or
if end < start.

```pycon
>>> BitStream('0xc3e').find('0b1111')
(6,)
```

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.rfind
documentation:
Find final occurrence of substring bs.

Returns a single item tuple with the bit position if found, or an
empty tuple if not found. The bit position (pos property) will
also be set to the start of the substring if it is found.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
start -- The bit position to end the reverse search. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the first bit to reverse search.
       Defaults to len(self).
bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be found on byte
               boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty, if start < 0, if end > len(self) or
if end < start.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.read
documentation:
Interpret next bits according to the format string and return result.

fmt -- Token string describing how to interpret the next bits.

Token examples: 'int:12'    : 12 bits as a signed integer
                'uint:8'    : 8 bits as an unsigned integer
                'float:64'  : 8 bytes as a big-endian float
                'intbe:16'  : 2 bytes as a big-endian signed integer
                'uintbe:16' : 2 bytes as a big-endian unsigned integer
                'intle:32'  : 4 bytes as a little-endian signed integer
                'uintle:32' : 4 bytes as a little-endian unsigned integer
                'floatle:64': 8 bytes as a little-endian float
                'intne:24'  : 3 bytes as a native-endian signed integer
                'uintne:24' : 3 bytes as a native-endian unsigned integer
                'floatne:32': 4 bytes as a native-endian float
                'hex:80'    : 80 bits as a hex string
                'oct:9'     : 9 bits as an octal string
                'bin:1'     : single bit binary string
                'ue'        : next bits as unsigned exp-Golomb code
                'se'        : next bits as signed exp-Golomb code
                'uie'       : next bits as unsigned interleaved exp-Golomb code
                'sie'       : next bits as signed interleaved exp-Golomb code
                'bits:5'    : 5 bits as a bitstring
                'bytes:10'  : 10 bytes as a bytes object
                'bool'      : 1 bit as a bool
                'pad:3'     : 3 bits of padding to ignore - returns None

fmt may also be an integer, which will be treated like the 'bits' token.

The position in the bitstring is advanced to after the read items.

Raises ReadError if not enough bits are available.
Raises ValueError if the format is not understood.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.readlist
documentation:
Interpret next bits according to format string(s) and return list.

fmt -- A single string or list of strings with comma separated tokens

: describing how to interpret the next bits in the bitstring. Items
  can also be integers, for reading new bitstring of the given length.

kwargs -- A dictionary or keyword-value pairs - the keywords used in the

: format string will be replaced with their given value.

The position in the bitstring is advanced to after the read items.

Raises ReadError is not enough bits are available.
Raises ValueError if the format is not understood.

See the docstring for 'read' for token examples. 'pad' tokens are skipped
and not added to the returned list.

```pycon
>>> h, b1, b2 = s.readlist('hex:20, bin:5, bin:3')
>>> i, bs1, bs2 = s.readlist(['uint:12', 10, 10])
```

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.readto
documentation:
Read up to and including next occurrence of bs and return result.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be
               found on byte boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty.
Raises ReadError if bs is not found.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.peek
documentation:
Interpret next bits according to format string and return result.

fmt -- Token string describing how to interpret the next bits.

The position in the bitstring is not changed. If not enough bits are
available then all bits to the end of the bitstring will be used.

Raises ReadError if not enough bits are available.
Raises ValueError if the format is not understood.

See the docstring for 'read' for token examples.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.peeklist
documentation:
Interpret next bits according to format string(s) and return list.

fmt -- One or more integers or strings with comma separated tokens describing
       how to interpret the next bits in the bitstring.
kwargs -- A dictionary or keyword-value pairs - the keywords used in the
          format string will be replaced with their given value.

The position in the bitstring is not changed. If not enough bits are
available then all bits to the end of the bitstring will be used.

Raises ReadError if not enough bits are available.
Raises ValueError if the format is not understood.

See the docstring for 'read' for token examples.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.bytealign
documentation:
Align to next byte and return number of skipped bits.

Raises ValueError if the end of the bitstring is reached before
aligning to the next byte.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.ConstBitStream.__getitem__
documentation: Return a new bitstring representing a slice of the current bitstring.

A container or stream holding an immutable sequence of bits.

For a mutable container use the BitStream class instead.

Methods inherited from Bits:

all() -- Check if all specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
any() -- Check if any of specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
copy() -- Return a copy of the bitstring.
count() -- Count the number of bits set to 1 or 0.
cut() -- Create generator of constant sized chunks.
endswith() -- Return whether the bitstring ends with a sub-string.
find() -- Find a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
findall() -- Find all occurrences of a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
fromstring() -- Create a bitstring from a formatted string.
join() -- Join bitstrings together using current bitstring.
pp() -- Pretty print the bitstring.
rfind() -- Seek backwards to find a sub-bitstring.
split() -- Create generator of chunks split by a delimiter.
startswith() -- Return whether the bitstring starts with a sub-bitstring.
tobitarray() -- Return bitstring as a bitarray from the bitarray package.
tobytes() -- Return bitstring as bytes, padding if needed.
tofile() -- Write bitstring to file, padding if needed.
unpack() -- Interpret bits using format string.

Other methods:

bytealign() -- Align to next byte boundary.
peek() -- Peek at and interpret next bits as a single item.
peeklist() -- Peek at and interpret next bits as a list of items.
read() -- Read and interpret next bits as a single item.
readlist() -- Read and interpret next bits as a list of items.
readto() -- Read up to and including next occurrence of a bitstring.

Special methods:

Also available are the operators [], ==, !=, +, *, ~, <<, >>, &, |, ^.

Properties:

[GENERATED_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTIONS]

len -- Length of the bitstring in bits.
pos -- The current bit position in the bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.__init__
documentation:
Either specify an 'auto' initialiser:
A string of comma separated tokens, an integer, a file object,
a bytearray, a boolean iterable or another bitstring.

Or initialise via **kwargs with one (and only one) of:
bin -- binary string representation, e.g. '0b001010'.
hex -- hexadecimal string representation, e.g. '0x2ef'
oct -- octal string representation, e.g. '0o777'.
bytes -- raw data as a bytes object, for example read from a binary file.
int -- a signed integer.
uint -- an unsigned integer.
float / floatbe -- a big-endian floating point number.
bool -- a boolean (True or False).
se -- a signed exponential-Golomb code.
ue -- an unsigned exponential-Golomb code.
sie -- a signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
uie -- an unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
floatle -- a little-endian floating point number.
floatne -- a native-endian floating point number.
bfloat / bfloatbe - a big-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatle -- a little-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatne -- a native-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
intbe -- a signed big-endian whole byte integer.
intle -- a signed little-endian whole byte integer.
intne -- a signed native-endian whole byte integer.
uintbe -- an unsigned big-endian whole byte integer.
uintle -- an unsigned little-endian whole byte integer.
uintne -- an unsigned native-endian whole byte integer.
filename -- the path of a file which will be opened in binary read-only mode.

Other keyword arguments:
length -- length of the bitstring in bits, if needed and appropriate.
          It must be supplied for all integer and float initialisers.

          ignored and this is intended for use when
          initialising using 'bytes' or 'filename'.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.__copy__
documentation: Return a new copy of the BitStream.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.__iadd__
documentation:
Append to current bitstring. Return self.

bs -- the bitstring to append.

The current bit position will be moved to the end of the BitStream.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.prepend
documentation:
Prepend a bitstring to the current bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to prepend.

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.__delitem__
documentation:
Delete item or range.

```pycon
>>> a = BitStream('0x001122')
>>> del a[8:16]
>>> print a
0x0022
```

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.insert
documentation:
Insert bitstring at bit position pos.

bs -- The bitstring to insert.
pos -- The bit position to insert at.

The current bit position will be moved to the end of the inserted section.
Raises ValueError if pos < 0 or pos > len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bitstream.BitStream.replace
documentation:
Replace all occurrences of old with new in place.

Returns number of replacements made.

old -- The bitstring to replace.
new -- The replacement bitstring.
start -- Any occurrences that start before this will not be replaced.
         Defaults to 0.
end -- Any occurrences that finish after this will not be replaced.
       Defaults to len(self).
count -- The maximum number of replacements to make. Defaults to
         replace all occurrences.
bytealigned -- If True replacements will only be made on byte
               boundaries.

Raises ValueError if old is empty or if start or end are
out of range.

A container or stream holding a mutable sequence of bits

Subclass of the ConstBitStream and BitArray classes. Inherits all of
their methods.

Methods:

all() -- Check if all specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
any() -- Check if any of specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
append() -- Append a bitstring.
bytealign() -- Align to next byte boundary.
byteswap() -- Change byte endianness in-place.
clear() -- Remove all bits from the bitstring.
copy() -- Return a copy of the bitstring.
count() -- Count the number of bits set to 1 or 0.
cut() -- Create generator of constant sized chunks.
endswith() -- Return whether the bitstring ends with a sub-string.
find() -- Find a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
findall() -- Find all occurrences of a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
fromstring() -- Create a bitstring from a formatted string.
insert() -- Insert a bitstring.
invert() -- Flip bit(s) between one and zero.
join() -- Join bitstrings together using current bitstring.
overwrite() -- Overwrite a section with a new bitstring.
peek() -- Peek at and interpret next bits as a single item.
peeklist() -- Peek at and interpret next bits as a list of items.
pp() -- Pretty print the bitstring.
prepend() -- Prepend a bitstring.
read() -- Read and interpret next bits as a single item.
readlist() -- Read and interpret next bits as a list of items.
readto() -- Read up to and including next occurrence of a bitstring.
replace() -- Replace occurrences of one bitstring with another.
reverse() -- Reverse bits in-place.
rfind() -- Seek backwards to find a sub-bitstring.
rol() -- Rotate bits to the left.
ror() -- Rotate bits to the right.
set() -- Set bit(s) to 1 or 0.
split() -- Create generator of chunks split by a delimiter.
startswith() -- Return whether the bitstring starts with a sub-bitstring.
tobitarray() -- Return bitstring as a bitarray from the bitarray package.
tobytes() -- Return bitstring as bytes, padding if needed.
tofile() -- Write bitstring to file, padding if needed.
unpack() -- Interpret bits using format string.

Special methods:

Mutating operators are available: [], <<=, >>=, +=, *=, &=, |= and ^=
in addition to [], ==, !=, +, *, ~, <<, >>, &, | and ^.

Properties:

[GENERATED_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTIONS]

len -- Length of the bitstring in bits.
pos -- The current bit position in the bitstring.

Either specify an 'auto' initialiser:
A string of comma separated tokens, an integer, a file object,
a bytearray, a boolean iterable, an array or another bitstring.

Or initialise via **kwargs with one (and only one) of:
bin -- binary string representation, e.g. '0b001010'.
hex -- hexadecimal string representation, e.g. '0x2ef'
oct -- octal string representation, e.g. '0o777'.
bytes -- raw data as a bytes object, for example read from a binary file.
int -- a signed integer.
uint -- an unsigned integer.
float / floatbe -- a big-endian floating point number.
bool -- a boolean (True or False).
se -- a signed exponential-Golomb code.
ue -- an unsigned exponential-Golomb code.
sie -- a signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
uie -- an unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code.
floatle -- a little-endian floating point number.
floatne -- a native-endian floating point number.
bfloat / bfloatbe - a big-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatle -- a little-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
bfloatne -- a native-endian bfloat format 16-bit floating point number.
intbe -- a signed big-endian whole byte integer.
intle -- a signed little-endian whole byte integer.
intne -- a signed native-endian whole byte integer.
uintbe -- an unsigned big-endian whole byte integer.
uintle -- an unsigned little-endian whole byte integer.
uintne -- an unsigned native-endian whole byte integer.
filename -- the path of a file which will be opened in binary read-only mode.

Other keyword arguments:
length -- length of the bitstring in bits, if needed and appropriate.
          It must be supplied for all integer and float initialisers.

          ignored and this is mainly intended for use when

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__copy__
documentation: Return a new copy of the Bits for the copy module.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__add__
documentation:
Concatenate bitstrings and return new bitstring.

bs -- the bitstring to append.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__radd__
documentation:
Append current bitstring to bs and return new bitstring.

bs -- An object that can be 'auto' initialised as a bitstring that will be appended to.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__getitem__
documentation:
Return a new bitstring representing a slice of the current bitstring.

Indices are in units of the step parameter (default 1 bit).
Stepping is used to specify the number of bits in each item.

```pycon
>>> print(BitArray('0b00110')[1:4])
'0b011'
>>> print(BitArray('0x00112233')[1:3:8])
'0x1122'
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__len__
documentation: Return the length of the bitstring in bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__str__
documentation:
Return approximate string representation of bitstring for printing.

Short strings will be given wholly in hexadecimal or binary. Longer
strings may be part hexadecimal and part binary. Very long strings will
be truncated with '...'.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__repr__
documentation:
Return representation that could be used to recreate the bitstring.

If the returned string is too long it will be truncated. See __str__().

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__eq__
documentation:
Return True if two bitstrings have the same binary representation.

```pycon
>>> BitArray('0b1110') == '0xe'
True
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__ne__
documentation:
Return False if two bitstrings have the same binary representation.

```pycon
>>> BitArray('0b111') == '0x7'
False
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__invert__
documentation:
Return bitstring with every bit inverted.

Raises Error if the bitstring is empty.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__lshift__
documentation:
Return bitstring with bits shifted by n to the left.

n -- the number of bits to shift. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__rshift__
documentation:
Return bitstring with bits shifted by n to the right.

n -- the number of bits to shift. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__mul__
documentation:
Return bitstring consisting of n concatenations of self.

Called for expression of the form 'a = b*3'.
n -- The number of concatenations. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__rmul__
documentation:
Return bitstring consisting of n concatenations of self.

Called for expressions of the form 'a = 3*b'.
n -- The number of concatenations. Must be >= 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__and__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'and' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '&' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__rand__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'and' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- the bitstring to '&' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__or__
documentation:
Bit-wise 'or' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '|' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

documentation:
Bit-wise 'or' between two bitstrings. Returns new bitstring.

bs -- The bitstring to '|' with.

Raises ValueError if the two bitstrings have differing lengths.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__xor__

bs -- The bitstring to '^' with.

Return whether bs is contained in the current bitstring.

documentation: Return an integer hash of the object.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.__bool__
documentation: Return False if bitstring is empty, otherwise return True.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._clear
documentation: Reset the bitstring to an empty state.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setauto_no_length_or_offset
documentation: Set bitstring from a bitstring, file, bool, array, iterable or string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setauto
documentation: Set bitstring from a bitstring, file, bool, array, iterable or string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setfile
documentation: Use file as source of bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setbytes
documentation: Set the data from a bytes or bytearray object.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setbytes_with_truncation
documentation: Set the data from a bytes or bytearray object, with optional offset and length truncations.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getbytes
documentation: Return the data as an ordinary bytes object.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getbytes_printable
documentation: Return an approximation of the data as a string of printable characters.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setuint
documentation: Reset the bitstring to have given unsigned int interpretation.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getuint
documentation: Return data as an unsigned int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setint
documentation: Reset the bitstring to have given signed int interpretation.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getint
documentation: Return data as a two's complement signed int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setuintbe
documentation: Set the bitstring to a big-endian unsigned int interpretation.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getuintbe
documentation: Return data as a big-endian two's complement unsigned int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setintbe
documentation: Set bitstring to a big-endian signed int interpretation.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getintbe
documentation: Return data as a big-endian two's complement signed int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getuintle
documentation: Interpret as a little-endian unsigned int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getintle
documentation: Interpret as a little-endian signed int.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getfloatbe
documentation: Interpret the whole bitstring as a big-endian float.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getfloatle
documentation: Interpret the whole bitstring as a little-endian float.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setue
documentation:
Initialise bitstring with unsigned exponential-Golomb code for integer i.

Raises CreationError if i < 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._readue
documentation:
Return interpretation of next bits as unsigned exponential-Golomb code.

Raises ReadError if the end of the bitstring is encountered while
reading the code.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setse
documentation: Initialise bitstring with signed exponential-Golomb code for integer i.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._readse
documentation:
Return interpretation of next bits as a signed exponential-Golomb code.

Advances position to after the read code.

Raises ReadError if the end of the bitstring is encountered while
reading the code.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setuie
documentation:
Initialise bitstring with unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code for integer i.

Raises CreationError if i < 0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._readuie
documentation:
Return interpretation of next bits as unsigned interleaved exponential-Golomb code.

Raises ReadError if the end of the bitstring is encountered while
reading the code.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setsie
documentation: Initialise bitstring with signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code for integer i.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._readsie
documentation:
Return interpretation of next bits as a signed interleaved exponential-Golomb code.

Advances position to after the read code.

Raises ReadError if the end of the bitstring is encountered while
reading the code.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setbin_safe
documentation: Reset the bitstring to the value given in binstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setbin_unsafe
documentation: Same as _setbin_safe, but input isn't sanity checked. binstring mustn't start with '0b'.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getbin
documentation: Return interpretation as a binary string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._setoct
documentation: Reset the bitstring to have the value given in octstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getoct
documentation: Return interpretation as an octal string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._sethex
documentation: Reset the bitstring to have the value given in hexstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._gethex
documentation:
Return the hexadecimal representation as a string.

Raises an InterpretError if the bitstring's length is not a multiple of 4.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._getlength
documentation: Return the length of the bitstring in bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._copy
documentation: Create and return a new copy of the Bits (always in memory).

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._slice
documentation: Used internally to get a slice, without error checking.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._absolute_slice
documentation:
Used internally to get a slice, without error checking.
Uses MSB0 bit numbering even if LSB0 is set.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._readtoken
documentation: Reads a token from the bitstring and returns the result.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._addright
documentation: Add a bitstring to the RHS of the current bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._addleft
documentation: Prepend a bitstring to the current bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._truncateleft
documentation: Truncate bits from the start of the bitstring. Return the truncated bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._truncateright
documentation: Truncate bits from the end of the bitstring. Return the truncated bits.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._insert
documentation: Insert bs at pos.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._overwrite
documentation: Overwrite with bs at pos.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._delete
documentation: Delete bits at pos.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._reversebytes
documentation: Reverse bytes in-place.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._invert
documentation: Flip bit at pos 1<->0.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._invert_all
documentation: Invert every bit.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._ilshift
documentation: Shift bits by n to the left in place. Return self.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._irshift
documentation: Shift bits by n to the right in place. Return self.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._imul
documentation: Concatenate n copies of self in place. Return self.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._validate_slice
documentation: Validate start and end and return them as positive bit positions.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.unpack
documentation:
Interpret the whole bitstring using fmt and return list.

fmt -- A single string or a list of strings with comma separated tokens
       describing how to interpret the bits in the bitstring. Items
       can also be integers, for reading new bitstring of the given length.
kwargs -- A dictionary or keyword-value pairs - the keywords used in the
          format string will be replaced with their given value.

Raises ValueError if the format is not understood. If not enough bits
are available then all bits to the end of the bitstring will be used.

See the docstring for 'read' for token examples.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.find
documentation:
Find first occurrence of substring bs.

Returns a single item tuple with the bit position if found, or an
empty tuple if not found. The bit position (pos property) will
also be set to the start of the substring if it is found.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
start -- The bit position to start the search. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the last bit to search.

> Defaults to len(self).

bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be

: found on byte boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty, if start < 0, if end > len(self) or
if end < start.

```pycon
>>> BitArray('0xc3e').find('0b1111')
(6,)
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._find_msb0
documentation: Find first occurrence of a binary string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.findall
documentation:
Find all occurrences of bs. Return generator of bit positions.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
start -- The bit position to start the search. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the last bit to search.
       Defaults to len(self).
count -- The maximum number of occurrences to find.
bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be found on
               byte boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty, if start < 0, if end > len(self) or
if end < start.

Note that all occurrences of bs are found, even if they overlap.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.rfind
documentation:
Find final occurrence of substring bs.

Returns a single item tuple with the bit position if found, or an
empty tuple if not found. The bit position (pos property) will
also be set to the start of the substring if it is found.

bs -- The bitstring to find.
start -- The bit position to end the reverse search. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the first bit to reverse search.
       Defaults to len(self).
bytealigned -- If True the bitstring will only be found on byte
               boundaries.

Raises ValueError if bs is empty, if start < 0, if end > len(self) or
if end < start.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._rfind_msb0
documentation: Find final occurrence of a binary string.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.cut
documentation:
Return bitstring generator by cutting into bits sized chunks.

bits -- The size in bits of the bitstring chunks to generate.
start -- The bit position to start the first cut. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the last bit to use in the cut.
       Defaults to len(self).
count -- If specified then at most count items are generated.
         Default is to cut as many times as possible.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.split
documentation:
Return bitstring generator by splitting using a delimiter.

The first item returned is the initial bitstring before the delimiter,
which may be an empty bitstring.

delimiter -- The bitstring used as the divider.
start -- The bit position to start the split. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position one past the last bit to use in the split.
       Defaults to len(self).
count -- If specified then at most count items are generated.
         Default is to split as many times as possible.
bytealigned -- If True splits will only occur on byte boundaries.

Raises ValueError if the delimiter is empty.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.join
documentation:
Return concatenation of bitstrings joined by self.

sequence -- A sequence of bitstrings.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.tobytes
documentation:
Return the bitstring as bytes, padding with zero bits if needed.

Up to seven zero bits will be added at the end to byte align.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.tobitarray
documentation: Convert the bitstring to a bitarray object.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.tofile
documentation:
Write the bitstring to a file object, padding with zero bits if needed.

Up to seven zero bits will be added at the end to byte align.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.startswith
documentation:
Return whether the current bitstring starts with prefix.

prefix -- The bitstring to search for.
start -- The bit position to start from. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position to end at. Defaults to len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.endswith
documentation:
Return whether the current bitstring ends with suffix.

suffix -- The bitstring to search for.
start -- The bit position to start from. Defaults to 0.
end -- The bit position to end at. Defaults to len(self).

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.all
documentation:
Return True if one or many bits are all set to bool(value).

value -- If value is True then checks for bits set to 1, otherwise
         checks for bits set to 0.
pos -- An iterable of bit positions. Negative numbers are treated in
       the same way as slice indices. Defaults to the whole bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.any
documentation:
Return True if any of one or many bits are set to bool(value).

value -- If value is True then checks for bits set to 1, otherwise
         checks for bits set to 0.
pos -- An iterable of bit positions. Negative numbers are treated in
       the same way as slice indices. Defaults to the whole bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.count
documentation:
Return count of total number of either zero or one bits.

value -- If bool(value) is True then bits set to 1 are counted, otherwise bits set

: to 0 are counted.

```pycon
>>> Bits('0xef').count(1)
7
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._chars_per_group
documentation: How many characters are needed to represent a number of bits with a given format.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._bits_per_char
documentation: How many bits are represented by each character of a given format.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._pp
documentation: Internal pretty print method.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.pp
documentation:
Pretty print the bitstring's value.

fmt -- Printed data format. One or two of 'bin', 'oct', 'hex' or 'bytes'.

: The number of bits represented in each printed group defaults to 8 for hex and bin,
  12 for oct and 32 for bytes. This can be overridden with an explicit length, e.g. 'hex:64'.
  Use a length of 0 to not split into groups, e.g. `bin:0`.

width -- Max width of printed lines. Defaults to 120. A single group will always be printed

: per line even if it exceeds the max width.

sep -- A separator string to insert between groups. Defaults to a single space.
show_offset -- If True (the default) shows the bit offset in the first column of each line.
stream -- A TextIO object with a write() method. Defaults to sys.stdout.

```pycon
>>> s.pp('hex16')
>>> s.pp('b, h', sep='_', show_offset=False)
```

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.copy
documentation: Return a copy of the bitstring.

methods.name: bitstring.bits.Bits.fromstring
documentation: Create a new bitstring from a formatted string.

class_variables.name: bitstring.bits.Bits._unprintable
documentation: ASCII control characters

A container holding an immutable sequence of bits.

For a mutable container use the BitArray class instead.

Methods:

all() -- Check if all specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
any() -- Check if any of specified bits are set to 1 or 0.
copy() - Return a copy of the bitstring.
count() -- Count the number of bits set to 1 or 0.
cut() -- Create generator of constant sized chunks.
endswith() -- Return whether the bitstring ends with a sub-string.
find() -- Find a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
findall() -- Find all occurrences of a sub-bitstring in the current bitstring.
fromstring() -- Create a bitstring from a formatted string.
join() -- Join bitstrings together using current bitstring.
pp() -- Pretty print the bitstring.
rfind() -- Seek backwards to find a sub-bitstring.
split() -- Create generator of chunks split by a delimiter.
startswith() -- Return whether the bitstring starts with a sub-bitstring.
tobitarray() -- Return bitstring as a bitarray from the bitarray package.
tobytes() -- Return bitstring as bytes, padding if needed.
tofile() -- Write bitstring to file, padding if needed.
unpack() -- Interpret bits using format string.

Special methods:

Also available are the operators [], ==, !=, +, *, ~, <<, >>, &, |, ^.

Properties:

[GENERATED_PROPERTY_DESCRIPTIONS]

len -- Length of the bitstring in bits.
